channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Gauteng Transport Department Urges Taxi Operators to Collect Licences by August 14 to Avoid Cancellation
item.description: The Gauteng Department of Roads and Transport has issued a warning to public transport operators, particularly minibus taxi and scholar transport services, urging them to collect their operating licen...

item.title: Sandra Dacha Emphasizes Faith and Friendship Through Prayer Circle
item.description: Kenyan actress Sandra Dacha shared a heartfelt video of her prayer gathering with close friends, highlighting the importance of spiritual connections. The clip, posted on Instagram, shows women in a w...

item.title: Zanzibar's Political Accord Promotes Peace and Prosperity
item.description: A landmark political reconciliation agreement between Tanzania’s ruling Chama Cha Mapinduzi (CCM) and opposition party ACT Wazalendo in Zanzibar has been hailed as a turning point for the semi-autonom...

item.title: B.C. Sees Sharp Rise in Drowning Deaths, Safety Groups Urge Life Jacket Use
item.description: British Columbia has recorded the sharpest increase in drowning deaths this year, with at least 30 fatalities reported in 2026 compared to 20 in the same period in 2025, representing a 50% rise. The B...

item.title: Manipur farmer killed by armed Naga group; police probe ongoing
item.description: A 53-year-old farmer, Haolal Singsit, was killed by unidentified armed individuals suspected to be from an armed Naga group in Manipur's Govanjang village on Saturday. The incident occurred around 5:3...

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham Dies at 71 After Sudden Illness
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, a prominent Republican senator from South Carolina, passed away at the age of 71 following a sudden illness. The announcement was made by his office, which described the cause of ...

item.title: Former Qatar Emir Sheikh Hamad Al-Thani Dies at 74, Shaped Nation's Modernization
item.description: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani, the former ruler of Qatar, passed away at the age of 74. His leadership marked a transformative era for Qatar, steering the country from a traditional emirate to a g...

item.title: INEC extends deadline for candidate submissions in 2027 polls
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) has extended the deadline for political parties to submit their candidates for the 2027 presidential and National Assembly elections by three days....

item.title: Cuba Faces Back-to-Back Nationwide Blackouts Amid Power Grid Crisis
item.description: Cuba experienced a nationwide blackout affecting nearly 10 million people on Friday, marking the second such event in just a week. The crisis stems from a deteriorating power grid and fuel shortages e...

item.title: Serotonin and Heart Valve Disease: New Research Links Genetic Factors and Medications
item.description: A study led by Columbia University researchers reveals a potential connection between serotonin, commonly known for its role in mood regulation, and the progression of degenerative mitral regurgitatio...

item.title: IShowSpeed Reveals Rare Cristiano Ronaldo Football Boots Gift
item.description: IShowSpeed, a popular streamer, recently revealed a pair of ultra-rare Nike Mercurial Superfly 11 'CR7 Gold Scorpion' boots gifted to him by Cristiano Ronaldo during the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the US....

item.title: Philippines Honors Legal Victory in West Philippine Sea Amid Ongoing Disputes
item.description: The Philippine Navy commemorated the 10th anniversary of the 2016 arbitral ruling in the West Philippine Sea with a synchronized water salute and tugboat horn ceremony in Subic Bay Freeport. This even...

item.title: India condemns attack on commercial vessel off Oman as search continues for missing Indian crew member
item.description: India has strongly condemned the attack on the commercial vessel GFS Galaxy off the coast of Oman, describing the continuing attacks on commercial shipping in the Middle East as deeply worrisome. Acco...

item.title: Tanzania's Pentecostal Churches Launch Fund to Support Construction Projects
item.description: The Tanzania Pentecostal Churches Association (TPA) has launched a dedicated fund to support church construction projects nationwide, addressing the challenge of inadequate worship facilities faced by...

item.title: Argentina advances to World Cup semi-finals after defeating Switzerland
item.description: Argentina booked their place in the World Cup semi-finals after a hard-fought 3-1 victory over Switzerland in extra time. Lionel Scaloni’s team took an early lead in the 10th minute through Alexis Mac...

item.title: A comprehensive guide to the most iconic and controversial World Cup kits of all time
item.description: The article explores the history and design of World Cup football kits, highlighting standout examples such as Uruguay's vibrant home strip and a notorious Puma-designed Swiss kit that sparked controv...

item.title: Jude Bellingham defends England's performance after World Cup quarter-final victory against Norway
item.description: England secured a 2-1 victory over Norway in their World Cup quarter-final, with Jude Bellingham scoring both goals to send them into the semi-finals. Manager Thomas Tuchel expressed dissatisfaction w...

item.title: MP woman gang-raped by masked intruders during robbery attempt, police investigate
item.description: A 40-year-old tribal woman in Madhya Pradesh's Alirajpur district was allegedly gang-raped by masked men who broke into her home during a suspected robbery. The incident occurred on Friday night when ...

item.title: Lindsey Graham, Key Trump Ally and South Carolina Senator, Passes Away at 71
item.description: Prominent US Senator Lindsey Graham, a loyal supporter of former President Donald Trump, has died at 71 following a brief illness. The Republican senator from South Carolina passed away on July 11, 20...

item.title: UK Shops Implement Facial Recognition to Alert Police on Offenders, Raising Civil Liberties Concerns
item.description: The article discusses the deployment of facial recognition technology in UK retail stores by Facewatch, a system used by major chains like Sainsbury's and Spar. The technology will soon allow real-tim...
